data.content:
### Use an Installed Plugin Command

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins

This example demonstrates how to execute a command provided by an installed plugin. Plugin commands are namespaced by the plugin's name (e.g., 'commit-commands'). This specific command, '/commit-commands:commit', stages changes, generates a commit message, and creates a commit.

```shell
/commit-commands:commit
```

--------------------------------

### Install Test Plugin

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces

Install a test plugin from a specific marketplace to verify that the marketplace is loading correctly and plugins can be installed. This step is crucial for end-to-end testing of your marketplace setup.

```shell
/plugin install test-plugin@marketplace-name
```

--------------------------------

### Start Using Claude Code

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/index

Initializes Claude Code in a project directory. After navigating to your project, running the 'claude' command will prompt for login and then activate the tool.

```bash
cd your-project
claude

```

--------------------------------

### Install a Plugin

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins

Installs a specified plugin from a marketplace. By default, it installs to the user scope. You can also specify the installation scope using the `--scope` option.

```shell
/plugin install plugin-name@marketplace-name
```

```shell
claude plugin install formatter@your-org --scope project
```

--------------------------------

### Example Telemetry Configurations (Bash)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage

Provides example environment variable settings for various telemetry configurations in Claude Code. These examples cover console debugging, OTLP/gRPC, Prometheus exporters, multiple exporter setups, and specific configurations for metrics and logs with different endpoints. Telemetry is enabled via CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1.

```bash
# Console debugging (1-second intervals)
export CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1
export OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER=console
export OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_INTERVAL=1000
```

```bash
# OTLP/gRPC
export CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1
export OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER=otlp
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=grpc
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:4317
```

```bash
# Prometheus
export CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1
export OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER=prometheus
```

```bash
# Multiple exporters
export CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1
export OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER=console,otlp
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=http/json
```

```bash
# Different endpoints/backends for metrics and logs
export CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1
export OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER=otlp
export OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER=otlp
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL=http/protobuf
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT=http://metrics.company.com:4318
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_PROTOCOL=grpc
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_ENDPOINT=http://logs.company.com:4317
```

```bash
# Metrics only (no events/logs)
export CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1
export OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER=otlp
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=grpc
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:4317
```

```bash
# Events/logs only (no metrics)
export CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1
export OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER=otlp
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=grpc
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:4317
```

--------------------------------

### Complex Skill with Multiple Files (YAML & Python)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills

This example illustrates a multi-file Skill for PDF processing. It includes a main `SKILL.md` for overview and quick start, `FORMS.md` for form details, `REFERENCE.md` for API info, and utility scripts in a `scripts/` directory. The `allowed-tools` field restricts available tools.

```yaml
---
name: pdf-processing
description: Extract text, fill forms, merge PDFs. Use when working with PDF files, forms, or document extraction. Requires pypdf and pdfplumber packages.
allowed-tools: Read, Bash(python:*)
---

# PDF Processing

## Quick start

Extract text:
```python
import pdfplumber
with pdfplumber.open("doc.pdf") as pdf:
    text = pdf.pages[0].extract_text()
```

For form filling, see [FORMS.md](FORMS.md).
For detailed API reference, see [REFERENCE.md](REFERENCE.md).

```

```python
# scripts/fill_form.py
# Utility to populate form fields
import pypdf

def fill_form(pdf_path, data):
    # Implementation for filling form fields
    pass

```

```python
# scripts/validate.py
# Checks PDFs for required fields
def validate_pdf(pdf_path):
    # Implementation for validating PDF fields
    pass

```

--------------------------------

### Starting Claude Code in a Project

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/setup

Navigates to a project directory and starts the Claude Code CLI.

```bash
cd your-awesome-project
claude
```

--------------------------------

### Install LSP plugins for code intelligence

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins

These commands demonstrate how to install Language Server Protocol (LSP) plugins for various programming languages. These plugins enhance Claude's understanding of your codebase by providing features like definition jumping and type error detection. Ensure the corresponding language server binary is installed on your system.

```shell
/plugin install clangd-lsp@claude-plugins-official
```

```shell
/plugin install csharp-lsp@claude-plugins-official
```

```shell
/plugin install gopls-lsp@claude-plugins-official
```

```shell
/plugin install jdtls-lsp@claude-plugins-official
```

```shell
/plugin install lua-lsp@claude-plugins-official
```

```shell
/plugin install php-lsp@claude-plugins-official
```

```shell
/plugin install pyright-lsp@claude-plugins-official
```

```shell
/plugin install rust-analyzer-lsp@claude-plugins-official
```

```shell
/plugin install swift-lsp@claude-plugins-official
```

```shell
/plugin install typescript-lsp@claude-plugins-official
```

--------------------------------

### Install Plugin from Local Marketplace

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces

Command to install a specific plugin ('test-plugin') from a locally added marketplace ('my-local-marketplace').

```shell
/plugin install test-plugin@my-local-marketplace
```

--------------------------------

### Example Log Entry

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide

This is an example of an entry that would appear in the `bash-command-log.txt` file after a command like `ls` is executed by Claude Code and logged by the hook. It shows the command and its description.

```text
ls - Lists files and directories
```

--------------------------------

### Simple Skill Structure (YAML)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills

This example demonstrates a minimal Skill requiring only a `SKILL.md` file. It includes frontmatter with `name` and `description`, followed by instructions and best practices for the Skill's functionality.

```yaml
---
name: generating-commit-messages
description: Generates clear commit messages from git diffs. Use when writing commit messages or reviewing staged changes.
---

# Generating Commit Messages

## Instructions

1. Run `git diff --staged` to see changes
2. I'll suggest a commit message with:
   - Summary under 50 characters
   - Detailed description
   - Affected components

## Best practices

- Use present tense
- Explain what and why, not how

```

--------------------------------

### Plugin Installation using CLI

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference

Demonstrates how to install a Claude plugin using the `claude plugin install` command. It shows installation to user (default), project, and local scopes, specifying the plugin name and optionally the marketplace.

```bash
# Install to user scope (default)
claude plugin install formatter@my-marketplace

# Install to project scope (shared with team)
claude plugin install formatter@my-marketplace --scope project

# Install to local scope (gitignored)
claude plugin install formatter@my-marketplace --scope local
```

--------------------------------

### Install a Plugin from the Command Line

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins

This command installs a specific plugin, 'commit-commands', from the 'anthropics-claude-code' marketplace directly via the command line. The '@' symbol is used to specify the marketplace scope for the plugin.

```shell
/plugin install commit-commands@anthropics-claude-code
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Automatic Dependency Installation with SessionStart Hooks (JSON)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web

This JSON configuration demonstrates how to set up automatic dependency installation for a project using Claude Code. It defines a 'SessionStart' hook that triggers a shell script to install npm and pip packages. This is useful for ensuring all necessary dependencies are available when a new session begins.

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [
      {
        "matcher": "startup",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "\"$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\"/scripts/install_pkgs.sh"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Native Installation Script for Windows CMD

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/setup

Installs Claude Code using a CMD command to download, execute, and then clean up an installation script.

```batch
curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.cmd -o install.cmd && install.cmd && del install.cmd
```

--------------------------------

### Install npm and pip Packages using a Bash Script (Bash)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web

This bash script is designed to be executed as part of a 'SessionStart' hook in Claude Code. It performs a standard 'npm install' followed by 'pip install -r requirements.txt', ensuring that both Node.js and Python dependencies are installed. The script concludes with 'exit 0' to indicate successful execution.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
npm install
pip install -r requirements.txt
exit 0
```

--------------------------------

### Advanced Plugin Configuration Example

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces

An example of a comprehensive plugin entry, including custom paths for commands, agents, hooks, MCP servers, and other metadata. Sets 'strict' to false, meaning the plugin is fully defined by this entry.

```json
{
  "name": "enterprise-tools",
  "source": {
    "source": "github",
    "repo": "company/enterprise-plugin"
  },
  "description": "Enterprise workflow automation tools",
  "version": "2.1.0",
  "author": {
    "name": "Enterprise Team",
    "email": "enterprise@example.com"
  },
  "homepage": "https://docs.example.com/plugins/enterprise-tools",
  "repository": "https://github.com/company/enterprise-plugin",
  "license": "MIT",
  "keywords": ["enterprise", "workflow", "automation"],
  "category": "productivity",
  "commands": [
    "./commands/core/",
    "./commands/enterprise/",
    "./commands/experimental/preview.md"
  ],
  "agents": ["./agents/security-reviewer.md", "./agents/compliance-checker.md"],
  "hooks": {
    "PostToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Write|Edit",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/validate.sh"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "mcpServers": {
    "enterprise-db": {
      "command": "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/servers/db-server",
      "args": ["--config", "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/config.json"]
    }
  },
  "strict": false
}
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Marketplaces

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins

CLI commands for managing marketplaces. This includes listing all configured marketplaces, updating plugin listings from a specific marketplace, and removing a marketplace.

```shell
/plugin marketplace list
```

```shell
/plugin marketplace update marketplace-name
```

```shell
/plugin marketplace remove marketplace-name
```

--------------------------------

### Install ripgrep for search and discovery

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/troubleshooting

This section provides installation commands for `ripgrep`, a fast line-oriented search tool. Installing `ripgrep` is recommended for improving the performance and reliability of Claude Code's search and discovery features, especially for large codebases. After installation, you may need to set an environment variable.

```bash
# macOS (Homebrew)  
brew install ripgrep

# Windows (winget)
winget install BurntSushi.ripgrep.MSVC

# Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt install ripgrep

# Alpine Linux
apk add ripgrep

# Arch Linux
pacman -S ripgrep
```

--------------------------------

### Execute Installed Plugin Command

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces

The command to execute the installed `/review` plugin after it has been added and installed via the marketplace. This is typically run after selecting code in the editor.

```shell
/review
```

--------------------------------

### Add a Plugin Marketplace from GitHub

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins

This command adds a plugin marketplace hosted on GitHub. It requires the repository in the format 'owner/repo'. The system then downloads the marketplace catalog, making its plugins available for use.

```shell
/plugin marketplace add anthropics/claude-code
```

--------------------------------

### Install plugin from official Anthropic marketplace

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins

This command installs a plugin from the official Anthropic marketplace. Ensure you have the correct plugin name and marketplace identifier. The official marketplace is automatically available in Claude Code.

```shell
/plugin install plugin-name@claude-plugins-official
```

--------------------------------

### Add and Install Local Marketplace and Plugin

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces

Commands to add a local plugin marketplace and subsequently install a specific plugin from it. This enables the use of the installed plugin within the Claude Code environment.

```shell
/plugin marketplace add ./my-marketplace
/plugin install review-plugin@my-plugins
```

--------------------------------

### Install Claude Code (PowerShell)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/setup

Installs Claude Code using PowerShell. It leverages a PowerShell script for installation, allowing for specific version targeting.

```powershell
& ([scriptblock]::Create((irm https://claude.ai/install.ps1))) 1.0.58
```

--------------------------------

### Install Python Packages for Skills

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills

Lists the Python packages required for a Skill to function. These packages must be installed in the environment before Claude can utilize the Skill.

```bash
pip install pypdf pdfplumber
```

--------------------------------

### strictKnownMarketplaces - Exact Matching Examples

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings

Illustrates the strict matching requirements for Git-based sources (`github`, `git`) within `strictKnownMarketplaces`. It shows examples of sources that do NOT match due to differences in 'repo', 'ref', or 'path'.

```json
// These are DIFFERENT sources:
{ "source": "github", "repo": "acme-corp/plugins" }
{ "source": "github", "repo": "acme-corp/plugins", "ref": "main" }

// These are also DIFFERENT:
{ "source": "github", "repo": "acme-corp/plugins", "path": "marketplace" }
{ "source": "github", "repo": "acme-corp/plugins" }
```

--------------------------------

### Custom File Suggestion Command Script Example

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings

An example bash script demonstrating how to process the JSON input from Claude Code for file suggestion. It extracts the query, uses a hypothetical file index tool, and outputs newline-separated file paths to stdout, limited to 20 entries.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
query=$(cat | jq -r '.query')
your-repo-file-index --query "$query" | head -20
```

--------------------------------

### MCP Command Examples

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/slash-commands

These examples demonstrate how to invoke MCP commands, both without and with arguments. The second example shows passing a specific issue number and the third demonstrates creating a Jira issue with a title and priority.

```shell
> /mcp__github__list_prs
> /mcp__github__pr_review 456
> /mcp__jira__create_issue "Bug title" high
```

--------------------------------

### Add a Plugin Marketplace from a Local Marketplace JSON File

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins

This command adds a plugin marketplace by directly specifying the path to a local `marketplace.json` file. This method provides a precise way to load a marketplace from a specific file location on your system.

```shell
/plugin marketplace add ./path/to/marketplace.json
```

--------------------------------

### Control Claude Output Format (Bash)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/common-workflows

Configure Claude's output format for seamless integration with scripts and tools. Examples include plain text output, JSON for detailed message logs, and stream-JSON for real-time, incremental responses. Ensure the 'claude' CLI is installed and the desired input file exists.

```bash
cat data.txt | claude -p 'summarize this data' --output-format text > summary.txt
```

```bash
cat code.py | claude -p 'analyze this code for bugs' --output-format json > analysis.json
```

```bash
cat log.txt | claude -p 'parse this log file for errors' --output-format stream-json
```

--------------------------------

### Example settings.json Configuration

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings

This JSON snippet demonstrates a typical structure for a settings.json file in Claude Code. It includes configurations for permissions, environment variables, and company announcements. Permissions define allowed and denied actions, while environment variables can be used to control Claude Code's behavior.

```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [
      "Bash(npm run lint)",
      "Bash(npm run test:*)",
      "Read(~/.zshrc)"
    ],
    "deny": [
      "Bash(curl:*)",
      "Read(./.env)",
      "Read(./.env.*)",
      "Read(./secrets/**)"
    ]
  },
  "env": {
    "CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY": "1",
    "OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER": "otlp"
  },
  "companyAnnouncements": [
    "Welcome to Acme Corp! Review our code guidelines at docs.acme.com",
    "Reminder: Code reviews required for all PRs",
    "New security policy in effect"
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Migrate Hooks Configuration to Plugin

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins

This example shows how to migrate existing hook configurations from a .claude/settings.json file into a dedicated hooks.json file within the plugin. This allows hooks to be managed as part of the plugin's distributable assets.

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PostToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Write|Edit",
        "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "npm run lint:fix $FILE" }]
      }
    ]
  }
}

```

--------------------------------

### Environment Variable Persistence Example (.env format)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web

This example illustrates the format for specifying environment variables as key-value pairs, adhering to the .env file standard. These variables can be persisted for subsequent bash commands within Claude Code sessions by writing them to a file specified by the CLAUDE_ENV_FILE environment variable.

```text
API_KEY=your_api_key
DEBUG=true
```

--------------------------------

### Add a Plugin Marketplace from a Remote URL

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins

This command adds a plugin marketplace hosted at a remote URL, pointing directly to a `marketplace.json` file. This allows for integrating marketplaces from web servers or content delivery networks.

```shell
/plugin marketplace add https://example.com/marketplace.json
```

--------------------------------

### Add a Plugin Marketplace from a Local Directory

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins

This command adds a plugin marketplace located in a local directory. It assumes the directory contains a `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` file. This is useful for developing or using local marketplaces.

```shell
/plugin marketplace add ./my-marketplace
```

--------------------------------

### Add a Plugin Marketplace from a Git URL (SSH)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins

This command adds a plugin marketplace from any Git repository accessible via an SSH URL. It's compatible with different Git hosts and downloads the marketplace catalog after execution.

```shell
/plugin marketplace add git@gitlab.com:company/plugins.git
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Plugin State

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins

Commands to manage the state of installed plugins. This includes disabling, re-enabling, or completely uninstalling a plugin. The `--scope` option can be used to target a specific installation scope.

```shell
/plugin disable plugin-name@marketplace-name
```

```shell
/plugin enable plugin-name@marketplace-name
```

```shell
/plugin uninstall plugin-name@marketplace-name
```

```shell
claude plugin uninstall formatter@your-org --scope project
```

--------------------------------

### Start using Claude Code

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview

This command initiates the Claude Code assistant within your project directory. After running this command, you will be prompted to log in if it's your first time using the tool.

```bash
cd your-project
claude
```

--------------------------------

### Skill Permission Rule Examples

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/slash-commands

These examples show how to define permission rules for the Skill tool. 'Exact match' allows only a specific command with no arguments, while 'Prefix match' allows a command with any arguments.

```shell
# Allows only /commit with no arguments
Skill(/commit)

# Allows /review-pr with any arguments
Skill(/review-pr:*)
```

--------------------------------

### Add a Plugin Marketplace from a Git URL (HTTPS)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins

This command adds a plugin marketplace from any Git repository accessible via an HTTPS URL. It supports various Git hosting services like GitLab and Bitbucket. The marketplace catalog is downloaded upon execution.

```shell
/plugin marketplace add https://gitlab.com/company/plugins.git
```

--------------------------------

### Install Claude Code using Native Methods

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview

These commands install Claude Code directly on your system using different shell environments. Ensure you have a Claude.ai or Claude Console account before proceeding. These scripts download and execute the installation process.

```bash
curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
```

```powershell
irm https://claude.ai/install.ps1 | iex
```

```batch
curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.cmd -o install.cmd && install.cmd && del install.cmd
```

--------------------------------

### Start Claude as an MCP stdio Server (Bash)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp

This command starts Claude as a standard input/output (stdio) MCP server, allowing other applications to connect to it. The configuration requires specifying the command and arguments, ensuring the 'claude' executable is in the system's PATH or providing its full path.

```bash
# Start Claude as a stdio MCP server
claude mcp serve
```

```bash
# Find the full path to the claude executable
which claude
```

--------------------------------

### File Suggestion Command Input Example

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings

Illustrates the JSON input format that a custom file suggestion command receives via stdin. It includes a 'query' field representing the user's input for file path autocompletion.

```json
{"query": "src/comp"}
```

--------------------------------

### Prepend nvm Node Binaries to PATH in WSL

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/troubleshooting

This command explicitly adds the Node.js binaries managed by nvm to your system's PATH environment variable. By prepending this directory, you ensure that the Linux versions of Node.js and npm take priority over any Windows installations when running commands in WSL.

```bash
export PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/$(node -v)/bin:$PATH"
```

--------------------------------

### Example of Markdown Code Block with Language Tag

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/troubleshooting

This is an example of a correctly formatted markdown code block with the 'javascript' language tag, ensuring proper syntax highlighting.

```markdown
```javascript
function example() {
  return "hello";
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Plugin.json Example

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference

An example of a `plugin.json` file demonstrating semantic versioning. The version is specified in the `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` format.

```json
{
  "name": "my-plugin",
  "version": "2.1.0"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define Skill Functionality and Triggers

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills

Provides an example of a Skill description in YAML format. It details the Skill's capabilities and specifies trigger terms for Claude to use the Skill appropriately.

```yaml
theme={null}
description: Extract text and tables from PDF files, fill forms, merge documents. Use when working with PDF files or when the user mentions PDFs, forms, or document extraction.
```

--------------------------------

### Add a Plugin Marketplace from a Git URL with a Specific Branch/Tag

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins

This command allows adding a plugin marketplace from a Git repository using a specific branch or tag. It appends the reference (e.g., '#v1.0.0') to the Git URL, ensuring the correct version of the marketplace is fetched.

```shell
/plugin marketplace add https://gitlab.com/company/plugins.git#v1.0.0
```

--------------------------------

### Example of Markdown Code Block without Language Tag

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/troubleshooting

This is an example of a markdown code block generated by Claude Code that is missing a language identifier, which can lead to improper syntax highlighting.

```markdown
```
function example() {
  return "hello";
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### strictKnownMarketplaces - Allow Specific Marketplaces

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings

Example configuration for `strictKnownMarketplaces` in `managed-settings.json` that allows only a predefined list of GitHub and URL-based marketplaces. This enforces a strict allowlist policy.

```json
{ "strictKnownMarketplaces": [
  { "source": "github", "repo": "acme-corp/approved-plugins" },
  { "source": "github", "repo": "acme-corp/security-tools", "ref": "v2.0" },
  { "source": "url", "url": "https://plugins.example.com/marketplace.json" },
  { "source": "npm", "package": "@acme-corp/compliance-plugins" }
] }
```

--------------------------------

### Default Commit and PR Attribution Examples

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings

Provides examples of default attribution for git commits and pull requests generated by Claude Code. These include commit trailers and plain text for PR descriptions. The format can be customized or disabled.

```text
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

   Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

```text
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
```

--------------------------------

### Create Directory Structure for Local Marketplace

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces

Sets up the necessary directories for a local plugin marketplace and a sample plugin. This includes the main marketplace directory, a plugins subdirectory, and a commands subdirectory for the plugin.

```bash
mkdir -p my-marketplace/.claude-plugin
mkdir -p my-marketplace/plugins/review-plugin/.claude-plugin
mkdir -p my-marketplace/plugins/review-plugin/commands
```

--------------------------------

### Bash: Helper Function Approach Example

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/statusline

Demonstrates the beginning of a Bash script that reads JSON input once from stdin, intended for use with helper functions for more complex status line logic.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Read JSON input once
input=$(cat)

```

--------------------------------

### Managed MCP Configuration Example (JSON)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp

An example of the `managed-mcp.json` file used for exclusive control of MCP servers. This JSON structure defines a set of MCP servers, including their type, URL, command, arguments, and environment variables. This file is deployed by system administrators.

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "github": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/"
    },
    "sentry": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp"
    },
    "company-internal": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "/usr/local/bin/company-mcp-server",
      "args": ["--config", "/etc/company/mcp-config.json"],
      "env": {
        "COMPANY_API_URL": "https://internal.company.com"
      }
    }
  }
}

```

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/troubleshooting

This bash command retrieves the IP address assigned to your WSL2 instance, which is necessary for configuring Windows Firewall rules.

```bash
wsl hostname -I
```

### Create Custom Output Style - Markdown Example

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/output-styles

This snippet demonstrates the structure of a custom output style file using Markdown. It includes frontmatter for metadata such as the style's name and description, followed by the custom instructions for the assistant's behavior.

```markdown
```markdown  theme={null}
---
name: My Custom Style
description:
  A brief description of what this style does, to be displayed to the user
---

# Custom Style Instructions

You are an interactive CLI tool that helps users with software engineering
tasks. [Your custom instructions here...]

## Specific Behaviors

[Define how the assistant should behave in this style...]
```
```

### Write SKILL.md File Structure (YAML & Markdown)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills

The `SKILL.md` file is essential for defining a Claude Skill. It includes a YAML front matter for metadata like name and description, followed by Markdown instructions, and examples to guide Claude on its usage.

```yaml
---
name: your-skill-name
description: Brief description of what this Skill does and when to use it
---

# Your Skill Name

## Instructions
Provide clear, step-by-step guidance for Claude.

## Examples
Show concrete examples of using this Skill.
```

### Create Project-Specific Slash Command

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/slash-commands

This example demonstrates how to create a project-specific slash command. It involves creating a directory for commands and then writing the command's prompt to a Markdown file. This command will be available to all team members working on the project.

```bash
# Create a project command
mkdir -p .claude/commands
echo "Analyze this code for performance issues and suggest optimizations:" > .claude/commands/optimize.md
```

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide

This command is used to view the contents of the bash command log file created by the hook. It allows users to verify that commands are being logged as expected after Claude Code executes them.

```bash
cat ~/.claude/bash-command-log.txt
```

### Reset project-specific claude configuration

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/troubleshooting

These commands remove project-specific settings and MCP (Managed Cloud Platform) configurations for Claude Code. This is useful for isolating issues related to a particular project or for starting with a clean project configuration.

```bash
rm -rf .claude/
rm .mcp.json
```

### Restrict Tool Access using allowed-tools (YAML)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills

Limits the tools Claude can use within a specific Skill. This example restricts access to 'Read', 'Grep', and 'Glob' tools for a file reading skill.

```yaml
---
name: reading-files-safely
description: Read files without making changes. Use when you need read-only file access.
allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob
---

```

```yaml
---
name: reading-files-safely
description: Read files without making changes. Use when you need read-only file access.
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Grep
  - Glob
---

```

### Format TypeScript Files with Prettier

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide

This hook automatically formats TypeScript files after they are edited or written using the 'Edit' or 'Write' tool. It utilizes `prettier` to ensure consistent code style. The hook specifically targets files with the '.ts' extension.

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PostToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Edit|Write",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "jq -r '.tool_input.file_path' | { read file_path; if echo \"$file_path\" | grep -q '\\.ts$'; then npx prettier --write \"$file_path\"; fi; }"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins

This snippet demonstrates how to create the basic directory structure for a new Claude plugin and initialize its manifest file (plugin.json). The manifest contains essential metadata like the plugin's name, description, and version.

mkdir -p my-plugin/.claude-plugin

  "description": "Migrated from standalone configuration",
  "version": "1.0.0"
}

### Check Available Tools in Claude Code Environment

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web

This command allows you to inspect the pre-installed programming languages, versions, package managers, and development tools available within your Claude Code environment. It's useful for understanding the capabilities of your current setup.

```bash
check-tools

--------------------------------

### Send Desktop Notifications for User Input

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide

This hook triggers a desktop notification when Claude Code requires user input. It uses the `notify-send` command to display a message indicating that input is awaited. This hook is useful for non-interactive environments or to ensure prompt user attention.

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "Notification": [
      {
        "matcher": "",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "notify-send 'Claude Code' 'Awaiting your input'"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Extra Marketplaces for Team

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces

Add custom plugin marketplaces to your project's settings. This allows team members to be prompted to install required marketplaces when they trust the project folder. The configuration is stored in the `.claude/settings.json` file.

```json
{
  "extraKnownMarketplaces": {
    "company-tools": {
      "source": {
        "source": "github",
        "repo": "your-org/claude-plugins"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Slash Command Example: Review Prompt

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/slash-commands

Demonstrates a simple slash command for code review. This command is a single Markdown file intended for explicit manual invocation, such as `/review`.

```markdown
# .claude/commands/review.md
Review this code for:
- Security vulnerabilities
- Performance issues
- Code style violations
```

--------------------------------

### Claude Code Hooks Configuration

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide

This JSON snippet shows the configuration for Claude Code hooks, specifically for the `PreToolUse` event. It demonstrates how to define a matcher for 'Bash' commands and specifies a command hook to log these commands. This configuration is stored in user settings and applies globally.

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Bash",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "jq -r '\"\\\(.tool_input.command) - \\.\(.tool_input.description // \"No description\")\"' >> ~/.claude/bash-command-log.txt"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### CLAUDE.md File Import Example

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory

Demonstrates how to import additional files within a CLAUDE.md file using the '@path/to/import' syntax. This allows for modular instructions and can reference files in the user's home directory for personalized preferences.

```markdown
See @README for project overview and @package.json for available npm commands for this project.

# Additional Instructions
- git workflow @docs/git-instructions.md
```

```markdown
# Individual Preferences
- @~/.claude/my-project-instructions.md
```

```markdown
This code span will not be treated as an import: `@anthropic-ai/claude-code`
```

### Log Bash Commands with jq

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide

This hook logs shell commands executed by Claude Code, specifically filtering for Bash commands. It uses `jq` to parse the tool input and append the command and its description to a log file. This is useful for auditing and debugging command execution.

```bash
jq -r '"\(.tool_input.command) - \(.tool_input.description // "No description")"' >> ~/.claude/bash-command-log.txt
```

### Create Plugin Manifest

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins

Sets up the essential plugin manifest file (`plugin.json`) within the `.claude-plugin` directory. This JSON file defines the plugin's name, version, and description, which Claude uses for identification and display.

```bash
mkdir my-first-plugin/.claude-plugin
```

```json
{
"name": "my-first-plugin",
"description": "A greeting plugin to learn the basics",
"version": "1.0.0",
"author": {
"name": "Your Name"
}
}
```

### Encouraging Skill Tool Usage

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/slash-commands

To prompt Claude to use the Skill tool, reference the command directly in your prompts or configuration files. This example shows how to suggest running a unit test command when appropriate.

```shell
> Run /write-unit-test when you are about to start writing tests.
```

--------------------------------

### Configure LSP Server for a Language

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins

This JSON configuration defines how to run a Language Server Protocol (LSP) server for a specific programming language. It includes the command to execute the server, its arguments, and a mapping from file extensions to language identifiers. Ensure the specified language server binary is installed on the user's machine.

```json
{
  "go": {
    "command": "gopls",
    "args": ["serve"],
    "extensionToLanguage": {
      ".go": "go"
    }
  }
}
```

### Install Claude Code via PowerShell (Windows)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/index

Installs Claude Code on Windows using PowerShell to download and execute an installation script. This is a convenient method for Windows users.

```powershell
irm https://claude.ai/install.ps1 | iex

```

--------------------------------

### Example Plugin Manifest JSON

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference

A sample plugin manifest file (`plugin.json`) used to define plugin metadata, source location, commands, agents, and other configurations. It specifies the plugin's name, source directory, description, and paths to commands and agents.

```json
{
  "name": "my-plugin",
  "source": "./",
  "description": "Plugin that needs root-level access",
  "commands": ["./plugins/my-plugin/commands/"],
  "agents": ["./plugins/my-plugin/agents/"],
  "strict": false
}
```

### Install Claude Code via Homebrew (macOS)

Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/index

Installs Claude Code as a cask package using the Homebrew package manager. This is a straightforward method for macOS users who have Homebrew installed.

```shell
brew install --cask claude-code

```
